keyInsight: Sunshine Kebabs is bleeding margin in real time (18.1% vs 22.8% planned, 7 variations, $37k labour overrun) — intervene this week before it becomes the next Fenwicks Marina.

recommendations.title: Stop the bleed on Sunshine Kebabs East Maitland NOW
recommendations.evidence: Sunshine Kebabs (#251206, $205k, Chadd Hoffner) is tracking at 18.1% actual margin vs 22.8% planned, with $37,036 labour overrun and 7 variations (9.9% of contract). This is the exact pattern that sunk Fenwicks Marina (#251012): planned 27.4%, landed 20.3%, $8.4k labour overrun, 3 variations. The hospitality fitout type wins 92% of bids but Sunshine is now mid-build and slipping.
dataPoints: Sunshine Kebabs: planned margin 22.8% vs actual 18.1%, labourOverrun $37,036
Sunshine Kebabs: 7 variations = 9.9% of contract — highest variation count in active book
Fenwicks Marina archived at -7.1 margin gap with $8,376 labour overrun and 234-day build cycle
Sold Dec 2025 — 6 months in-flight already
recommendations.reasoning: This single job is the largest active margin risk in the portfolio. The variation count is climbing AND labour is overrunning — that's a scope-control failure, not just a labour-tracking issue. If left to drift it could erase $20-30k of expected profit.
recommendations.action: Convene a Sunshine Kebabs scope-freeze meeting this week: lock remaining variations, re-forecast labour to completion, and decide which client-requested changes get billed vs absorbed.
recommendations.impact: Protect ~$20-30k of planned margin and prevent a 4-5pt margin miss on SBI's largest active hospitality job.

recommendations.title: Chadd Hoffner is carrying 8 active jobs — overload risk is real
recommendations.evidence: Chadd Hoffner is PM on 8 of 15 active jobs AND all 9 archived jobs, with avg build cycle of 105 days and avg variation pct of 3.6%. Every other named PM (Leah Procter, Leah Carraro, Gemma King, Chadd Yorston, Michael Cook) carries just 1 active job each. Job #260509 (John Dosanjh) has PM = 'TBC' and #251213 Mingara has PM = 'PM' — placeholder assignments suggesting overflow.
dataPoints: Chadd Hoffner: 8 active + 9 archived jobs vs every other PM at 1
Active jobs unassigned: #260509 PM='TBC', #251213 PM='PM'
Coastal Physio (Hoffner) ran 333 build days with 9 variations and $13,906 labour overrun
Fenwicks Marina (Hoffner) ran 234 build days, -7.1 margin gap
recommendations.reasoning: The longest-running, most-variation-heavy archived jobs (Coastal Physio, Fenwicks, Bozmik 229 days) all sit with Hoffner. With 8 more active — including the $205k Sunshine Kebabs and $175k Karuah Internal — bandwidth is the single biggest threat to delivery margin in Q3.
recommendations.action: Reassign 2-3 smaller active jobs (Hungry Wolfs $3k, Taylors Conveyancing $20k, Wyoming Medical $4k) off Hoffner to Yorston or a Leah; formally allocate PM for #260509 and #251213 by Friday.
recommendations.impact: Free Hoffner to focus on $500k+ of larger high-margin work and shorten build cycles on smaller jobs.

recommendations.title: Planned margin of 23.1% is a default — not an estimate
recommendations.evidence: Seven jobs (Fletcher, Marine Rescue, Endo, Erina Sawmill, Display Cabinet, Hungry Wolfs, Natural Foot Podiatry, Lakehaven, Karuah Deck) all carry plannedMarginPct of exactly 23.1%. Yet actuals range from 22.5% (Display Cabinet) to 61.7% (Fletcher) and 35.9% (Endo). This isn't estimating — it's a copy-paste default that prevents real learning.
dataPoints: Tracey Fletcher: planned 23.1%, actual 61.7% (gap +38.6)
Marine Rescue: planned 23.1%, actual 54.1% (gap +31.0)
Display Cabinet Adolphus: planned 23.1%, actual 22.5% (gap -0.6)
Endo Water Damage: planned 23.1%, actual 35.9% (gap +12.8)
Hungry Wolfs, Natural Foot Podiatry, Lakehaven all also = 23.1% planned
recommendations.reasoning: When every small-medium build defaults to 23.1%, leadership can't tell over-runs from under-pricing. The Fletcher/Marine Rescue +30pt 'wins' are almost certainly under-recorded labour, not real profit — meaning the company is congratulating itself on phantom margin.
recommendations.action: Replace the 23.1% default in the estimating template with a type-banded planned margin (Joinery, Small Build, Fitout, Design) and require estimator sign-off on any override.
recommendations.impact: Surfaces real margin variance, ends false-positive 'wins', and gives PMs a credible budget to defend.

recommendations.title: Coastal Physio's labour pattern is repeating on Karuah Internal
recommendations.evidence: Coastal Physiotherapy (#240207) ran 333 build days, 9 variations, $13,906 labour overrun, sales cycle of 226 days — the longest in the book. Karuah Internal BUILD (#250522, $175k, Hoffner) sold May 2025 — now 372 days in-flight with 3 variations already and labour tracking $6,907 favourable (likely unrecorded). Bozmik Jillaby also ran 229 days.
dataPoints: Coastal Physio: 333 build days, 9 variations, $13,906 labour overrun
Karuah Internal: sold 2025-05-27, still active 12+ months later, 3 variations
Bozmik Jillaby: 229 build days, 4 variations
Erina Sawmill: 127 build days, 60 site days, $2,996 labour overrun
recommendations.reasoning: Long-tail jobs are where margin quietly evaporates — Hoffner's three longest jobs all overran labour. Karuah Internal is silently approaching the same cliff, and its 'too-good' actual margin (37.3% vs 22.3% planned) is the same labour-not-yet-coded signature.
recommendations.action: Schedule a hard milestone review on Karuah Internal this week: confirm completion date, true labour-to-go, and lock variation cutoff.
recommendations.impact: Avoid a repeat of the $14k Coastal Physio labour blowout on a job 23% larger.

recommendations.title: Pipeline is invisible — 29 deals worth $501k have no type tagged
recommendations.evidence: Pipeline by stage shows '(no type)' holding 29 deals worth $501,000 — that's 73% of total pipeline value sitting untagged. Typed pipeline is thin: only $80k Joinery (4 deals), $100k Hospitality (2 deals), $10k Office Fitout (3 deals). Yet Hospitality Fitout has a 91.7% win rate at $140k avg — SBI's most lucrative segment.
dataPoints: (no type) pipeline: 29 deals, $501,000
Hospitality Fitout win rate 91.7%, avg won value $140,075
Office Fitout win rate 73.2%, avg won value $46,825
Only 2 active Hospitality pipeline deals worth $100k
recommendations.reasoning: Leadership literally cannot tell what's in the pipeline. With Hospitality Fitout winning 11/12 bids at $140k each, even one untagged hospitality deal mis-prioritised is a $140k miss. The team is flying blind on the segment that pays best.
recommendations.action: Triage the 29 untyped pipeline deals this week — assign type to each, and immediately escalate any Hospitality or Office Fitout opportunities to closing focus.
recommendations.impact: Surface 2-4 hidden $100k+ opportunities and concentrate sales effort on highest-win-rate segments.

recommendations.title: Search-sourced leads convert at 32% — half of Existing Client (68%)
recommendations.evidence: Search source: 70 deals, 19 won, 41 lost = 31.7% win rate. Existing Client: 28 deals, 15 won = 68.2%. Referral: 46.2%. Yet Search delivers the highest avg won value ($65,894) outside the 1300 number. Web Form: 0 wins on 4 deals.
dataPoints: Search: 70 deals, 31.7% win rate, $65,894 avg won value
Existing Client: 28 deals, 68.2% win rate
Web Form: 4 deals, 0 won
Referral: 16 deals, 46.2% win rate
recommendations.reasoning: Search is SBI's largest top-of-funnel by volume but losing 2 of every 3 — and Web Form is 0-for-4. Either qualification is too loose (wasting estimating capacity) or the early-stage response/pitch isn't competitive. Meanwhile the existing-client channel is undermined.
recommendations.action: Add a 10-minute qualification call before any Search/Web-Form lead enters estimating; redirect saved capacity into proactive outreach to dormant Existing Clients.
recommendations.impact: Reduce estimating waste on the 41 Search losses and lift overall win-rate by 3-5 points.

recommendations.title: Top repeat clients have gone cold — Austral Herbs and Wyong Golf at risk
recommendations.evidence: Top client by value is Wyong Golf Club Fitout at $611,595 (1 deal). Austral Herbs Fountaindale is #2 at $527,872 across 2 deals. Neither appears in the active jobs list. Of 13 repeat clients, 7 have zero wins (Raine & Horne 4 deals 0 wins; McDonalds Morisset 3 deals 0 wins; OLG Planked Urban Oak 2/0; Cafe Fitout Newcastle 2/0).
dataPoints: Wyong Golf Club: $611,595 lifetime, not in active jobs
Austral Herbs: $527,872 across 2 deals, not in active jobs
Raine & Horne Rogers Group: 4 deals, 0 won
McDonalds Morisset Remodel: 3 deals, 0 won
Repeat clients with wins: only 4 of 13
recommendations.reasoning: Two clients represent over $1.1M of historical revenue and neither has a current job. Meanwhile multi-deal prospects (Raine & Horne, McDonalds) keep bidding and never converting — that's a pitch/pricing pattern, not bad luck.
recommendations.action: Chadd to personally re-engage Wyong Golf and Austral Herbs this fortnight with a tailored scope idea; do a post-mortem on the Raine & Horne and McDonalds losses to find the common objection.
recommendations.impact: Re-open two $500k+ relationships and stop wasting estimating effort on serial-losing repeat bidders.

recommendations.title: Mid-size deals ($75k-$300k) are SBI's win-rate trough
recommendations.evidence: Win rate by size: <$25k = 70%, $25-75k = 62%, $75-150k = 48%, $150-300k = 47%, $300k+ = 57%. The middle band is where SBI loses most — 19 losses across $75-300k vs 17 wins. Yet won jobs in this band include Erina Sawmill ($152k) and Bozmik ($119k), proving capability.
dataPoints: $300k+: 4 won / 3 lost (57%) — better than mid-band
recommendations.reasoning: SBI wins small and wins very large but stumbles in the middle — classic signal that mid-size bids face stronger competition and SBI's pitch isn't differentiating. With Sunshine Kebabs ($205k), Morisset ($166k) and Karuah ($175k) all in this band currently, fixing the mid-band pitch protects the next cohort.
recommendations.action: Run a loss-review on the 19 $75-300k losses from the last 12 months: identify the top 3 objections (price, timeline, references) and build a mid-band pitch deck addressing each.
recommendations.impact: Lift mid-band win rate from 47% toward 60% = ~4 extra wins/year at ~$120k average = $480k revenue.

patternsObserved.notes: Default planned margin of 23.1% appears on at least 9 jobs — this is template noise, not estimation, and it's masking both real wins and real losses. Multiple 'too good to be true' active margins (Morisset 95.1%, Karuah Deck 98.6%, Wyoming Medical 95.8%, Lauren Moore 100%) almost certainly reflect un-coded labour rather than real performance; treat any active margin >50% as a data-quality flag, not a celebration. Chadd Hoffner's name on 17 of 24 jobs is a single-point-of-failure that will become acute if Sunshine Kebabs, Karuah Internal, and Morisset all hit crunch in the same fortnight. Finally, the $501k of untyped pipeline alongside a hot 92% Hospitality win rate suggests SBI may be sitting on its highest-value segment without realising it.
